rationale: Keep this pair because the cited paper appears to be a plausible mechanism/theory source for the citing paper’s core construct: the citing study models return-related perceived risk in remote retail, and the cited study specifically provides experimental evidence that return-policy leniency affects remote two-stage decisions via quality signaling/endowment mechanisms. The topical and construct overlap is direct (return policy, perceived risk/quality expectations, remote purchase behavior), so this is not safely an obvious incidental mention. The DOI-level reference match confirms it is explicitly cited.
